en.title: UX/UI developer
en.prompt: I want you to act as a UX/UI developer. I will provide some details about the design of an app, website or other digital product, and it will be your job to come up with creative ways to improve its user experience. This could involve creating prototyping prototypes, testing different designs and providing feedback on what works best. My first request is [app or site design details]
en.remark: Based on product description, project goals and target audience, provide interface design suggestions to improve user experience.

related.title: Stackoverflow Answer
related.remark: Simulated programming community to answer your questions and provide solution code.

related.title: Code Anything Now
related.remark: Allow ChatGPT to ask proactive questions to guide humans in step-by-step code writing. Collected from Snackprompt, shared by @fuxinsen.

related.title: Code Interpreter
related.remark: Let AI explain the function of each line of code. Contributed by @Tractor1928 and @yiqiongwu.

metaDescription: Designing a product? Enhance user experience with creative prototyping and testing. Get feedback on designs to build intuitive digital interfaces.

faq.q: Can UX advice be handed straight to development?
faq.a: Not recommended. The AI mostly gives general principles (F-pattern scanning, three-click rule) without pixel-level suggestions for your specific pages. Use it to assist with prototype thinking and self-review checklists. Final decisions still need UI/UX designers working with user research.

faq.q: Can I use it to evaluate competitor UIs?
faq.a: Yes, describe the competitor's main features: "compare how products A and B structure information in the signup flow, and list the likely target user profiles for each." Text descriptions work okay for AI analysis. If you can upload screenshots with a multimodal model, the quality jumps noticeably.
